raw_consumer_statement: On XXXX XXXX XXXX, I attempted to book a hotel reservation and received a fraud alert stating the charge was declined. Believing the reservation failed, I immediately made a second booking for the same stay. Both charges later posted, resulting in a duplicate charge of {$2400.00}. After discovering the duplicate, we contacted the hotel directly and explained that the second booking was made only because of the fraud-decline alert, but the hotel refused to cancel or refund the duplicate charge. I then disputed the duplicate with Barclays. Barclays denied the dispute after accepting the merchants response, but neither the merchant nor Barclays addressed the fraud-decline alert that caused the duplicate booking. This was a processing error caused by issuer fraud controls, not an intentional second purchase.
